auth.signInSubtitle: Enterprise document audit — use your organization account.
auth.keycloakHint: Roles (admin, operator, viewer) are assigned in Keycloak.
auth.devModeOnLogin: Sign-in is disabled in this environment (OIDC not configured).
auth.devModeHint: OIDC is off — API uses dev admin access.
auth.forbiddenBody: Your account does not have permission for this action. Contact an administrator or switch account.
auth.errorConfiguration: Sign-in is not configured correctly. Verify OIDC issuer URLs, platform secrets, and that Keycloak is reachable from the edge.
auth.keycloakNotRunning: Keycloak is not reachable. Check the auth service and OIDC settings for this deployment.
auth.authServiceStarting: Sign-in service is starting. Wait a moment, then try again.
auth.errorAccessDenied: Access was denied. You may not have permission to use this application.
auth.errorVerification: Sign-in link expired or was already used.
auth.errorOAuth: Sign-in with Keycloak failed. Try again.

common.backendOffline: Backend offline — start Docker or run uvicorn

runProgress.gpuColdStartHint: Serverless GPU — the first request after idle can take 1–2 minutes while a worker starts.
runProgress.queueServerless: Runs are processed in order. Serverless GPU jobs may also wait 1–2 minutes on the first request after idle.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Audit outcomes, compliance trends, and workflows at a glance.
dashboard.pageDescription: Live view of audits, pass rates, and workflows that need attention.
dashboard.offlineHint: Start the platform to load audits and metrics. You can still create workflows offline.

performance.title: Audit volume
performance.hint: Click a day to filter audit runs.
performance.emptyHint: Run workflows to populate daily audit counts.
performance.summary: {total} audits this period. Peak day {peakDay} with {peakRuns} audits.

getStarted.title: Get your first audit running
getStarted.description: Follow the same three steps as the workflow builder — define fields, add rules, then test and deploy.
getStarted.footerHint: Completed audits and pass rates will appear on this dashboard.

platform.title: Platform pulse
platform.hint: Queue, workers, extraction runtime, and operator mode.
platform.queueIdle: No runs waiting or in progress

fleet.title: Workflow fleet
fleet.hint: Deployed APIs with live stats, plus drafts ready to finish.
fleet.liveApis: {count, plural, one {# live API} other {# live APIs}}

operator.title: Operator & benchmarks
operator.hint: Background jobs for model pull, warmup, and quality benchmarks.
operator.noJobs: No operator jobs yet — run a benchmark from Settings.

audits.title: Audit Reports
audits.description: Browse, filter, and triage every workflow run.

table.summary: {count, plural, =0 {No audits} one {# audit} other {# audits}}
table.empty: No audits match the current filters.

status.failedWithRules: Failed ({count, plural, one {# rule} other {# rules}})

users.title: Users & access
users.description: Manage team members, assign roles, and review platform permissions.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.description: Your data extraction and validation pipelines.

delete.confirmDescription: This will permanently remove {count} workflow(s) from your list. Runs and audit history are kept.
delete.confirmDescriptionNamed: Delete: {names}. Runs and audit history are kept.
delete.empty: No workflows yet. Create one to get started.

steps.blockedHint: Complete step 1 first (document name + at least one field)
steps.extractHint: Document types, fields, and extraction model
steps.rulesHint: Logic and LLM checks on extracted values
steps.uploadHint: Upload a sample → extract → validate in one run
steps.continueBlockedSchema: Name each document and add at least one field before continuing.
steps.continueRulesOptional: Add validation rules, or continue — rules are optional for extraction-only workflows.
steps.continueReady: When you're done with this step, continue to the next.
steps.finalStepHint: Run a test with sample files, then deploy when you're ready.

nameGate.title: Name your workflow
nameGate.hint: Choose a clear name your team will recognize before configuring documents and rules.
nameGate.label: Workflow name

builder.liveTestHint: Instant preview of extraction and rules against sample values.

test.uploadHint: Upload one file per document type. The test runs the selected extraction model, then validates the extracted values.
test.uploadFilesFirst: Upload at least one sample file to run OCR and validate your rules.
test.runningOcr: Document extraction, then logic and LLM validation…
test.noFilesHint: Upload files for real extraction, or run without files to validate rules on schema placeholders.
test.idleHint: Upload your sample files and click "Run test".
test.resultsHint: Extraction and rule results will appear here after running the test.
test.errorRunIdHint: Run ID — share with an operator or open the audit report for details.

progress.saveWorkflowDetail: Persisting your latest schema and rules on the server
progress.uploadCheckDetail: Detecting direct-to-storage upload vs API fallback
progress.uploadReuseDetail: Same files as the last run — skipping re-upload ({files})
progress.uploadPresignDetail: Requesting secure upload links from the API
progress.uploadConfirmDetail: Confirming files landed in storage and pass validation
progress.startRunDetail: Creating the audit run and dispatching to workers
progress.pollRunDetail: Document extraction, field mapping, and rule validation

deploy.sectionHint: Run a sample above, then deploy the same extraction and validation as an API endpoint for other documents.
deploy.readyHint: Your schema and rules are configured. Deploy to generate your live API endpoint. Callers upload one file per document slot — extraction fields are already defined in the workflow.
deploy.liveHint: Your API endpoint is active. Upload one file per document slot; tag each file with your configured document type names via document_types.
deploy.liveSubmitHint: POST returns 202 with a runId. Poll GET /api/v1/runs/{runId} for results. Reports also appear in Audit Reports.

ingestion.dropHint: Support for PDF, PNG, JPEG, and WebP (Max 50MB batch)
ingestion.fileHint: PDF, PNG, JPEG, WebP · drag & drop or click
ingestion.noDocsHint: Go to step 1 to define your document types and fields.

extraction.readPathHintDefault: Extract structured fields with the selected document model.
extraction.validationModeHintDefault: Logic rules run on extracted field values.
extraction.pathPlaceholder: Choose how to read and validate
extraction.pathHintDefault: Extract fields, then run validation rules on those values.
extraction.configurationUnavailableHint: The current workflow values are preserved. Reconnect to the API to change paths or models.
extraction.directModelLogicValidationHint: Repody VLM extracts schema fields directly. Logic rules run locally on the extracted values.
extraction.directModelLlmValidationHint: Repody VLM extracts schema fields directly. Optional LLM validation runs only when enabled on the platform.
extraction.markdownExtractionHint: Also run NuExtract document-to-Markdown for a readable preview. When off, run results show the raw structured JSON only.

docSource.title: Documents & fields to extract
docSource.hint: Name each document type, define its fields, and choose its extraction model.
docSource.documentTypePlaceholder: e.g. Invoice, Purchase Order, Expense Report
docSource.documentTypeHint: What kind of file will be uploaded for this document?
docSource.extractionInstructionsPlaceholder: Optional notes for the model, e.g. date format or section hints
docSource.extractionInstructionsHint: Sent to Repody with field descriptions. Use for document-wide rules, not per-field detail.

schema.title: What to extract
schema.description: Describe each piece of information you want pulled out of the document and choose how the value should be formatted.
schema.descriptionPlaceholder: What is this? e.g. the unique invoice identifier, usually near the header
schema.name: Name

verbatim-string.label: Exact text
verbatim-string.description: Copy text exactly as it appears in the document

string.label: Text
string.description: General text; light paraphrasing is allowed

time.label: Time
time.description: Time of day in ISO format

date-time.label: Date and time
date-time.description: Date and time in ISO format

currency.label: Currency code
currency.description: Three-letter currency code, e.g. EUR or USD

phone-number.label: Phone number
phone-number.description: Phone number in international or local format

duration.label: Duration
duration.description: A length of time in ISO format

country.label: Country code
country.description: Two-letter country code, e.g. FR or US

unit-code.label: Unit of measure
unit-code.description: Standard unit code, e.g. kg or m

schema.empty: No fields yet. Add one to describe what should be extracted from the document.

rules.title: Validation rules
rules.llmPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Verify that @total_amount is consistent with @subtotal and @tax_amount.
rules.emptyActive: No rules yet. Add a logic rule or LLM prompt below.
rules.hint: Choose logic or LLM when you add a rule. Each rule references fields from step 1 and runs on extracted values.
rules.runsOnOcr: Repody VLM extracts schema fields first. Logic checks run locally on the extracted values.
rules.validationEngineHint: LLM rules use the platform validation model when enabled. Extraction uses the selected document model.
rules.fieldReferenceHint: Reference extracted fields with @field_name. Only referenced values are sent to the validation model.
rules.noFieldsForPrompt: Select a document with configured fields first.
rules.validationModelUnavailable: Validation models could not be loaded. The platform default will be used.
rules.crossRequiresTwoDocs: Add a second document type in step 1 to enable cross-document rules.
rules.logicHint: Use field names above. Operators: == != > >= < <= AND OR NOT IN.

conditions.separateChecksHint: Each condition is validated separately — one pass/fail per check.
conditions.expressionIncomplete: Expression incomplete — pick a field and value for each condition.

api.testUploadHint: Upload a file for each document slot. The API uses your configured document type names in document_types, in the same order as files.
api.testUploadRequired: Upload at least one file to run extraction against your schema.
api.schemaHint: Extraction schema and validation rules are defined in this workflow — callers send files tagged with the document type names you configured (document_types), not field definitions.
api.pollHint: POST returns 202 with runId. Use document_types with your configured names (e.g. ["Invoice"]). Poll GET /api/v1/runs/{runId} for results.
api.runTestHint: Execute against your configured documents and see the full extraction report
api.processingHint: Extracting fields and validating rules for {name}
api.keyUnavailable: Full API key is only shown once at deploy — redeploy to run a live test from the UI, or paste a saved key below.

dryRun.title: Dry-run preview
dryRun.idle: Adjust the schema or rules to trigger a preview.
dryRun.idleHint: Rules need sample values or a test run with OCR — empty fields show as skipped, not failed.

toasts.nameRequired: Enter a workflow name before running a test.
toasts.draftSavedDetail: Your changes are stored on the server.
toasts.deployedDetail: Pipeline is now live in production.

settings.title: System Config
settings.description: Tune your workspace, integrations, and access.

general.workspaceHint: Displayed in the header and all exported reports.
general.regionHint: Where your data is stored and processed.
general.fiscalHint: Used to align audit date ranges with your fiscal calendar.
general.languageHint: Affects new sessions. Users can override per-session in the top bar.
general.timezoneHint: All timestamps are displayed in this timezone.

platform.banner: Runtime settings are loaded from environment configuration (AUDIT_* vars). The General tab shows live values from the API; other tabs are placeholders.
platform.title: Platform runtime
platform.readOnlyHint: To change these values, update Docker Compose or AUDIT_* environment variables and restart the API.

notifications.auditFailedDesc: Triggered when a scheduled or manual audit run produces failures.
notifications.slaBreachDesc: Triggered when an audit exceeds the configured review SLA.
notifications.newSubmissionDesc: Triggered when a new document enters the pipeline.

api.title: API Keys
api.description: Keys grant programmatic access to the Repody API.

health.degradedDetail: 3 consecutive failures in the last 30 minutes

stale.title: Runs stuck in progress
stale.detail: One or more audit runs have been running for over 15 minutes — check worker logs or open Audit Reports.
